observations.recommendation:
1.19 We recommended, and Management agreed, that the Local Chief Executive
(LCE) direct the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to:
i. Require all the COs to remit all their collections daily and in case
of collectors assigned to the field, at least once a week or as soon as the
collections reach ₱5,000.00 to the Municipal Treasurer and prepare
the RCD at the close of the business day or on the next working day;
ii. Deposit intact in the full amount of all collections remitted to him;
iii. Avoid issuing new sets of AFs until all collections were remitted
in full by the COs;
iv. Record all the transactions of Cash-Local Treasury Accounts to the
Cashbook-Cash in Treasury and reflect the correct balance of Cash on
hand at a certain date;
v. Ensure maintenance of all required Cashbooks and strictly monitor
that the COs’ Cashbook are updated and properly accomplished; and
vi. Instruct all the COs to ensure completeness and accuracy of the
information on the AFs and RCDs such as signatures and dates.
b. Direct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to intensify
their efforts to reconcile their records and effect appropriate adjustments
in their respective Cashbook and records, when necessary, to establish
the correct balance of the Cash-Local Treasury Account and other
affected accounts.

2.19 We recommended, and Management agreed, that the LCE require the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to:
i. Immediately reconcile the Cashbook balances with the GL to
ensure the accuracy and reliability of the CIB accounts; and
ii. Coordinate with the depository bank to ensure the prompt
delivery of the monthly bank statements or its equivalent to
facilitate the timely reconciliation of the CIB accounts.
b. Municipal Accountant-Designate to:
3. Prepare and maintain SLs for all bank accounts and submit to the
Audit Team the required subsidiary schedules of GL balances of
cash accounts for all funds and SLs per depository bank; and
4. Prepare and timely submit the monthly BRS for all accounts
maintained by the Municipality and ensure that balances
reflected in the BRS are reconciled with the balances as shown in
the GL.

3.11 We recommended, and Management agreed, that the LCE require:
a. Municipal Treasurer to:
i. Prepare its RPTAR regularly and furnish the MAO with a
certified list of taxpayers with the stated amount due and
collectible for the year; and
ii. Furnish the MAO with a certified list of all real property tax
delinquencies as the basis of the Municipal Accountant -Designate
for recording unrecorded tax delinquencies in the books.
b. Municipal Accountant-Designate to:
i. Set up the RPT/SET receivables at the beginning of the
accounting period based on the certified list of taxpayers due and
collectible for the year prepared by the Municipal Treasurer; and
ii. Prepare an SL for each taxpayer.
c. The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant-Designate to
reconcile their respective records pertaining to balances of RPT/SET
receivables.

4.16 We recommended, and Management agreed, that the LCE require the:
a. The Municipal Accountant-Designate to:
i. Prepare necessary adjusting entries to record the transfer of the
cost of completed projects amounting to ₱8,059,872.40 booked as PPE
under the TF to the GF;
ii. Prepare the necessary correcting entry to adjust the erroneous
booking of the PPE accounts, taking into consideration the
capitalization threshold of ₱50,000 for recognition as PPE as
provided in Section 4 of the Circular; and
iii. Prepare and maintain PLCs to ensure completeness of information in
the RCPPE and to account for the shortage and overage per items in
the PPE;
b. The Municipal Treasurer as the Property Officer to maintain PC as
prescribed under the MNGAS for LGUs.

4.17 Moreover, the LCE should mandate the Municipal Accountant-Designate to
furnish the auditor with a complete subsidiary schedule of PPE and lapsing
schedule for depreciation charges; provide SLs for the CIP account; create an
Inventory Committee who are temporarily relieved of all their regular duties,
to complete the physical inventory in three (3) months or less and to prepare
and submit the approved Physical Inventory Plan to the Audit Team, and
ensure full implementation of “One Time cleansing of PPE accounts” as
provided for in COA Circular 2020-006; and consider the salvage value of the
depreciable assets in computing for the depreciable expense as these, and the
above recommendations, are reiteration of the audit recommendations from
the previous audit.

5.15 We recommended, and Management agreed, that the LCE direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant-Designate to:
i. Draw the proposed JEV for a fair presentation of the financial
statements, taking into account that the 2022 books of accounts are
already closed:
Account
Account Title Debit Credit
Code
Accounts Payable 2-01-01-010 ₱335,680.00
Drugs and
1-04-04-060 ₱170,680.00
Medicine Inventory
Other PPE 1-07-99-990 150,000.00
Prior Period
3-01-02-010 15,000.00
Adjustment
ii. Discontinue the practice of recording the Accounts Payable unless
the goods/services have already been delivered or rendered, and
supported with complete documentation;
iii. Determine the validity of the said accounts and communicate with the
payees for the immediate payment/settlement of the accounts;
iv. Regularly check, monitor and keep the Financial Liabilities and
Other Payables accounts updated and adequate; and
v. Evaluate unclaimed obligations totaling ₱57,513.65 that remained
outstanding for two years or more. In the event that no actual claim
has been filed, whether administrative or judicial or payables are not
covered by perfected contracts or records, recommend for the
reversion of the same.
b. Municipal Treasurer and other concerned offices to coordinate with the
suppliers and employees to inform them of the unclaimed payables,
otherwise provide certification to the MAO that there are no valid
claimants for the long outstanding payables to facilitate the appropriate
reversion of funds.

6.9 We recommended, and Management agreed, that the LCE direct the Municipal
Accountant-Designate to:
a. Present the outstanding Loans Payable in the non-current liabilities
section of the Statement of Financial Position; and
b. Disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the necessary loan details
such as the purpose and funding sources of the loan, the terms and
conditions of the loan which includes the interest rate, maturity date and
quarterly/semi-annual amortization to enhance the understandability of
the users of financial statements.

7.18 We recommended, and Management agreed, that the LCE:
a. Direct MDRRMO to:
i. Judiciously prepare the AIP for LDRRMF incorporating time
schedules, availability of resources and other contingencies to avoid
delay in the implementation of PPAs; and
ii. Ensure and expedite the implementation of identified PPAs, to
strengthen the agency’s capacity in mitigating and preparing for,
responding to and recovering from the impact of disasters.
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant-Designate and the Municipal
Treasurer transfer the unexpended balances of the QRF and DRRMF-
MOOE for CY 2022 to the STF and ensure that, henceforth, Section
5.1.10 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 pertaining to transfers of
unexpended LDRRMF from the GF to TF are strictly observed;
c. Direct the Municipal Accountant-Designate to disclose in the Notes to
Financial Statements the details of the balances of unexpended LDRRMF
as shown in Annex D of COA Circular No. 2012-001 for fair presentation
and to enhance the understandability of the users of financial statements;
and
d. Require the MBO to prepare and maintain RAAO pertaining to the
LDRRMF for the current and previous years.

8.16 We recommended, and Management agreed, that the LCE direct the:
a. MHO to:
i. Assign the receipt, segregation, safekeeping and transfer to PHO
of the used and discarded vials functions to the Sanitation
Inspector.
ii. Require Sanitation Inspector to properly store and transfer the
vials together with the consolidated Form A – Reverse Logistic to
PHO every Friday of the vaccination week;
iii. Record all donations-in-kind received by the RHU in the
Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances,
duly supported with the (1) Acknowledgment Receipts, (2) proof
of receipt by and distribution to the beneficiaries not necessarily
in the required form, and (3) the inventory of remaining
undistributed items, if any; and
iv. Submit a copy of the same to the Storekeeper and the Municipal
Accountant-Designate.
b. Municipal Treasurer as the Property Officer to:
i. Assist the MHO in the inspection and acceptance of all donations-
in-kind, and in the conduct of inventory of the remaining
undistributed items, if any;
ii. Maintain copies of the Summary/List of Donations Received,
Distributed and Balances and its supporting documents; and
iii. Prepare and maintain a Registry of Donated COVID-19 Vaccines.
c. Municipal Accountant-Designate to make a disclosure in the Notes to
Financial Statements as to the quantity of received, issued and balance of
all donation-in-kind received by the Municipality, with categorization as
to food items, medicines and medical supplies, hygiene kits, and others
based on the Registry of Donated Relief Goods for DRRM.

9.23 We recommended, and Management agreed, that the LCE direct the:
a. Local Development Council, Implementing Office and Municipal
Planning Coordinator to:
i. Plan the procurement and implementation of the identified
priority development projects under the 20% DF to ensure the
completion of the PPAs;
ii. Coordinate with the beneficiaries/concerned offices with the PPAs
that will be included in the 20% DF AIP to determine specific
works to be done in each PPAs and to avoid delays in the
implementation; and
iii. Evaluate the feasibility of the particular PPA and observe
timeliness in the implementation to achieve the desired socio-
economic development and environmental outcomes.
b. Municipal Engineer and the BAC to periodically provide the MBO and the
Municipal Accountant with the status of projects and the
procurements undertaken by the Municipality to assist the concerned
offices in the periodic review and monitoring of the balances of current
and continuing appropriations and inform immediately the MBO and
Municipal Accountant whenever there are changes on the awarded PPAs
and problems in the project’s implementation to make the necessary
adjustment in the financial reports relative to the affected PPAs.

10.11 We recommended, and Management agreed, that the LCE to direct the
HRMO to:
a. Follow the staffing pattern as the basis for hiring JO and COS workers;
b. Prepare a valid Individual contract or MOA paid on a daily basis and the
period should not exceed 6 months;
c. Monitoring mechanism must be adopted in order to evaluate whether a JO
worker is qualified for the renewal of the contract; and
d. Submit a copy of quantified and detailed Individual Accomplishment
Reports to facilitate comparison with the job description stated on their
contracts, thereby, ensuring the protection of government funds from
unnecessary expenditures.

11.7 We recommended, and Management agreed, that the LCE direct the
Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant-Designate to immediately
release the respective Province and Barangays’ share. Henceforth, ensure the
on-time remittance of their share from RPT as provided under Section 271 (d)
of RA No. 7160.

We recommended, and the Management agreed, that the Local Chief
Executive (LCE) direct:
a. The Municipal Accountant – Designate to:
1. Immediately prepare and submit the BRS of all its depository bank
accounts together with the original copies of the bank statements,
take up reconciling items, and prepare necessary adjusting journal
entries;
2. Regularly reconcile the book balances and bank records vis-a-vis
the cashbook and prepare adjusting entries, if any; and
3. Regularly submit the mandatory reports and the supporting
documents within the reglementary period.
b. The Municipal Treasurer to:
1. Post the balances of the Cash in Bank – LCCA in the cashbook, with
proper segregation of the sources of funds; and
2. Coordinate with the depository bank to ensure the prompt delivery
of the monthly bank statements or its equivalent to facilitate the
timely reconciliation of the Cash in Bank accounts.

We recommended, and the Management agreed, that the LCE:
a. Require the Municipal Accountant – Designate to:
1. Furnish the Auditor with complete subsidiary schedule of PPE and
lapsing schedule for depreciation charges to facilitate the
computation of depreciation of the PPE;
2. Prepare Journal Entry Voucher to adjust the properties below the
capitalization threshold and ensure that all future acquisitions of
properties below the capitalization threshold of ₱15,000.00 will be
recorded in accordance with COA Circular 2020-006; and
3. Provide Subsidiary Ledgers for the CIP account.
b. Create an Inventory Committee, who are temporarily relieved of all their
regular duties, to complete the physical inventory in three (3) months or
less, and to prepare and submit the approved Physical Inventory Plan to
the COA Audit Team at least ten (10) calendar days before the scheduled
start of inventory-taking activities; and ensure full implementation of
“One-time cleansing of PPE accounts” as provided for in COA Circular
2020-006.

We recommended, and the Management agreed, that the LCE require:
a. The Municipal Accountant – Designate to:
1. Expedite the reconciliation of balances of Inter-Agency Payable
accounts and the remittance of monthly premium contributions of
the concerned employees to the aforesaid government agencies;
2. Account fully the prior year’s outstanding balance of the Inter-
agency payable accounts;
3. Analyze the Due to GOCCs account to determine the cause of
negative balances;
4. Reconcile the variance of ₱145,734.98 between the recorded amount
in the GL and the ORs;
5. Make the corresponding adjusting entry to correct the previous
entry made in relation to the payment of penalties/interests, to wit:
Due From Officers and Employees ₱16,962.04
Government Equity ₱16,962.04
b. The Municipal Treasurer to remit immediately the reconciled balance of
Inter-Agency Payable account as at December 31, 2021; and
c. The Municipal officials/employees concerned to return the amount of
₱16,962.04 to the government coffers as replacement of the penalties/
interests that should be borne by them.

We recommended, and the Management agreed, that the LCE require:
a. The concerned Municipal Health Officer (MHO) to submit immediately to
the audit team, thru the Municipal Accountant-Designate, the following
documents to establish their claim to AHDP and SRAs under AO Nos. 35,
36 and 42:
1. Authority for the entitlement of the allowance/office order/
authority to report for work;
2. Daily Time Records with Biometric printouts/copies of logbook or
Certificate of Attendance in the COVID Facility/Center by their
immediate supervisor during the period of claims;
3. Accomplishment reports indicating the number of COVID patients
attended to in a day.
4. For health workers not directly catering COVID-19 patients, an
explanation as to the scientific basis for the premise that healthcare
workers not directly catering to COVID-19 patients are equally
exposed to COVID-19 and can be considered “in contact with
COVID-19 patients”, and thus should be given the AHDP and SRA.
b. The Municipal Accountant to recognize Hazard Pay, Other Personnel
Benefits and the Other Maintenance and Operating Expenses Accounts by
₱178,363.64, ₱640,681.79 and ₱855,363.61, respectively, and the
corresponding Subsidy from NGAs in the total amount of ₱1,674,409.01 in
the payments of AHDP and SRA to permanent and contractual employees
in the CY 2021 Statement of Financial Performance.

We recommended, and the Management agreed, that the LCE:
a. Direct the Human Resource Management Officer to:
1. Refrain from approving request for leave monetization to
Municipal Officials and Personnel who has accumulated VL
Credits of below 15 days and not to allow monetization of the SL
Credits unless the employee will apply for the 50% monetization of
VL and SL credits duly supported with certification to justify the
validity of monetization; and
2. Submit the individual approved leave applications with certified
leave credit balances, request for leave and the detailed
computation of the 20 days monetized leave credits of LGU officials
and personnel thru the Municipal Accountant-Designate.
b. Require the Municipal Accountant – Designate to:
1. Submit the lacking supporting documents on the monetization of 20
days VL and SL Credits;
2. Reclassify the payments of leave monetization amounting to
₱2,345,613.90 from Terminal Leave Benefits to Other Personnel
Benefits in the CY 2021 Statement of Financial Performance.

We recommended, and the Management, thru the Municipal Planning and
Development Coordinator agreed to properly evaluate, determine the
expected output and properly plan the schedule of implementation of the
unimplemented development PPAs, in line with its Local Development Plan.

We further recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal
Planning Development Officer regularly monitor their implementation, in
accordance with the approved AIP of the Municipal Government.

We recommended and the Management agreed to require the Municipal
Accountant – Designate and other officials concerned to immediately submit
the DVs and its supporting documents totaling ₱1,959,170.15 for purposes of
review and audit.

Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant – Designate ensure that all
disbursement vouchers and their supporting documents, copies of cancelled
checks, if any, and other transaction documents are submitted on a regular
and timely basis.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a. Issue policies and guidelines in hiring JO Personnel in Legislative and
Executive Offices; and
b. Require the HRMO to:
1. Coordinate with the heads of the departments and offices to prepare a
contract incorporating a well-defined job description stating
therein the specific work to be assigned to each JO personnel; and
2. Submit a copy of Detailed Individual Accomplishment Reports to
facilitate comparison with the job description stated on their
contracts, thereby, ensuring the protection of government funds
from unnecessary expenditures.

We recommended, and the Management agreed to require the submission of
pre- and post-implementation documentary requirements relative to the
implementation of the TUPAD program to verify the validity and propriety of
payments.

We recommended, and the Management agreed to submit at the beginning of
the year a list of on-going and to be implemented PPAs for the current year,
and inform the COA within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure
project or before the start of the PPAs, that the appropriate project signboards
and/or public notices are already posted as required in COA Circular No.
2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

We recommended, and the Management agreed, that the LCE require:
a. The RCCs to prepared and submit to the MTO their individual monthly
RAAF;
b. The Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit the monthly Consolidated
RAAF for accountable forms with money value and without money value,
regularly and on time; and
c. The Municipal Accountant – Designate to closely monitor the submission
of the RCD with the Consolidated RAAF by the MTO to facilitate the
required monthly reconciliation of accounting records with the treasury
records.

1.7 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to:
a. require the Municipal Accountant- Designate to assess and validate
the collectability of the receivables and provide an allowance for
impairment based on the results of the assessment; and
b. require a regular review, reconciliation, and monitoring of all
booked receivables to ensure that only valid receivables are
recorded in the books. In addition, analyze the accounts that are
dormant and long outstanding and initiate the request for authority
to write-off of dormant receivables from the books pursuant to the
specific guidelines under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated
December 19, 2016.

2.8 We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the Municipal Accountant-
Designate to:
a. identify the cause of the discrepancies and effect immediate
adjustments in the books of accounts;
b. prepare monthly reconciliation of reciprocal accounts and ensure
that reconciling items are resolved and adjusted on a timely basis;
and
c. perform constant review and appraisal of transactions to deter
errors and omissions.

3.17 We recommended and the LCE agreed to:
a. create an Inventory Committee that will conduct physical count of
inventory items at least as of December 31, prepare RPCI and
reconcile the result of physical count with the property and
accounting records and submit RCPI to the Auditor not later than
January 31 of the following year; and
b. instruct the Municipal Treasurer to:
1. prepare and maintain SCs to record the receipt of procured
and issued supplies;
2. submit the distribution list with the supporting documents
for Agricultural and Marine Supplies, and Agricultural
Produce amounting to ₱1,167,750.00 and ₱4,802,200.00,
respectively, to the Municipal Accountant-Designate for
recording purposes;
3. verify the existence of the non-moving Textbooks and
Instructional Materials and Other Supplies and Materials
accounts amounting to ₱199,860.00 and ₱640,000.00,
respectively, and dispose the expired or obsolete supplies
and materials in accordance with the prescribed rules and
regulations on the disposal of government properties; and
4. oblige the requesting personnel to prepare the RIS for every
issuance of inventories, and prepare and submit the required
RSMIs with the RIS to the Municipal Accountant-Designate
for recording purposes.
c. require the Municipal Accountant-Designate to:
1. prepare and maintain SLCs to record the delivered
inventory items and the corresponding issuances; and
2. upon verification of the existence of non-moving inventories,
prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries.

4.13 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant-
Designate to:
a. effect the proposed adjusting journal entries in the LGU’s books of
accounts to correct the erroneous recording of the transactions and
the misstatement of the the accounts in the financial statements:
Due from LGUs 410,000.00
Government Equity 410,000.00
(To correct entry made for Financial Assistance granted for CY 2020)
b. ensure that financial transactions are properly recognized and
recorded in accordance with the Revised Chart of Accounts for
LGUs as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated
December 1, 2015;
c. comply with the documentary requirements under COA Circular
No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, in the subsequent fund transfers
before any funds shall be released; and
d. require the recipient LGU-Barangays to submit liquidation reports
to the accounting office for the recording thereof.

5.10 We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation and the LCE agreed to
require the Municipal Accountant-Designate and the Municipal Budget
Officer to maintain close coordination to come up with the SCBAA for CY
2020 and to submit the said report immediately to the Office of the Auditor;
and regularly thereafter, pursuant to COA Circular Nos. 2010-001 and 2016-
004, and paragraph 14 of PPSAS 24.

6.4 We recommended and Management agreed to use the quickest, most available,
and allowable mode of procurement under existing laws, rules, and
regulations to procure goods and services and infrastructure projects during
a state of calamity or emergency to effectively and efficiently address the
threat of any catastrophe or crisis.

7.8 We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer and
the Municipal Accountant-Designate to submit immediately the three DVs
totaling ₱3,562,029.73, together with their supporting documents.

8.10 We recommended and the Management agreed to require the Municipal
Accountant-Designate to submit to the Audit Team the lacking supporting
documents for the payment of hazard pay totaling ₱757,200.00 immediately,
for further evaluation of the transactions.

8.11 We also recommended and the Management to refrain from granting COVID-
19 hazard pay or other benefits in the absence of a legal basis or authority.

8.12 This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice(s) of
Disallowance, if warranted.

9.9 We recommended and the LCE agreed to:
a. establish a clear policy and procedures on the grant of AICS setting
forth specific guidelines on the use of funds and standard
procedures to properly implement the provision of AICS of the
LGU to its constituents; and
b. see to it that claims for AICS are duly supported with the required
documents before payment.

9.10 This observation is without prejudice to the inssuance of Notice of Suspension, if
warranted.

10.5 We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the BAC through its
Secretariat to submit the contract with bid documents for the eleven
infrastructure projects totaling ₱40,865,939.67.

10.6 We also reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendation and the
Management agreed to strictly adhere to COA Circular No. 2009-001, dated
February 12, 2009, on the timely submission of perfected contracts to avoid
administrative disciplinary action as provided for in section 127 of PD No.
1445.

11.10 We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. effect payment of goods and services through checks payable
directly to suppliers or dealers, and refrain from procuring goods
and services through reimbursement basis, except if the
disbursements are petty in nature and may be charged to Petty
Cash Fund; and
b. consider the setting-up of Petty Cash Fund duly supported by an
estimate of expenses which shall be strictly for petty operating
expenses in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002.

12.10 We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant-
Designate to submit immediately the lacking supporting documents
for the payment of monetization of leave credits and terminal leave
benefits totaling ₱2,069,883.69 and ₱944,472.92, respectively, to the
Audit Team, for review and validation;
b. require the 14 personnel to acknowledge the receipt of their claims
for monetization of leave credits by affixing their signatures on the
payroll sheet; and
c. henceforth, strictly adhere to COA Circular No. 2012-001 for the
documentary requirements for the payment of terminal leave and
monetization of leave credits.

13.9 We recommended and the LCE agreed to require:
a. The Department Heads and the SB Members to submit to the
Municipal Accountant-Designate, certifications that they did not
use or were not issued/assigned with any government vehicle duly
certified by the Municipal Treasurer;
b. The Department Heads to submit to the Municipal Accountant-
Designate, Approved DTRs or Certificates/evidences of services
rendered duly certified as correct by the HRMO;
c. The SB Secretary to submit to the Municipal Accountant-
Designate, certifications for the attendance of the SB Members to
sessions in CY 2020; and
d. The Municipal Accountant-Designate to submit to the Audit Team
the lacking documents submitted to him, for review purposes.

14.5 We recommended and the LCE agreed to:
a. issue an EO creating the M&E Team to assist the GFPS-TWG
Chairperson and MSWD in establishing a gender-responsive M&E
system; and
b. incorporate in the CY 2022 GAD Plan and Budget, the M&E
Team’s activities to gather the much-needed data and information
essential for establishing and enhancing the LGU GAD policies,
programs, and projects.

15.7 We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a. submit to the Auditor a status report on the actions taken on the
audit findings and recommendations embodied in the CY 2019
AAR using the prescribed form under COA Memorandum No.
2014-002 dated March 18, 2014; and
b. furnish a copy of the said report to DBM, the Speaker of the House
of Representatives, the President of the Senate of the Philippines,
the House Committee on Appropriations and the Senate
Committee on Finance, either in printed form or by way of
electronic document, as mandated by Section 89 of the General
Provisions of RA No. 11465.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to: (1) Prepare the subsidiary ledgers of the Cash in Bank – Local Currency, Current Account; (2) Prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statements on all the LGU bank accounts and submit the bank statements of the four bank accounts; and (3) Henceforth, prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statements for all bank accounts and submit the same together with the bank statements, paid checks, debit/credit memos and other related documents.
observations.reason: Still, no subsidiary ledgers were submitted. Still, no Bank Reconciliation Statements were submitted. Still Bank statements, paid checks, debit/credit memos and other related documents were not submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: (8) Municipal Accountant review thoroughly the entries made to its Loans Payable – Domestic account and prepare the necessary correcting entries thereafter.
observations.reason: No correcting entries were made by the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the: (16) require the Local Chief Executive: (16) require the Municipal Accountant to withhold retention money from progress payments and ensure that only claims with complete supporting documents are processed and paid. (17) require the BAC and Municipal Accountant to oblige the concerned contractor to post warranty security for the procurement of infrastructure project that had already been fully paid. (18) strictly observe the mandatory posting of warranty security for the contractor to perform his responsibilities pursuant to Section 62 of the IRR of RA 9184 and Section 9.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.reason: (17) Still the Municipal Accountant failed to require the contractor to post warranty security for the infrastructure project that had already been fully paid. (18) Still the Municipal Accountant failed to require the contractor to post Warranty security pursuant to Section 62 of the IRR of RA 9184 and Section 9.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for (19) strict observance of the guidelines on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund as provided for in DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.
observations.reason: Guidelines on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund were not strictly followed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: (21) The Sangguniang Bayan immediately revise the Municipal Revenue Code of 2005; (22) The LGU make use of the water meters bought in CY 2016 and require consumers to execute a written application/contract to be filed with the Office of the Municipal Mayor to avail the waterworks system services; (23) The Municipal Treasurer to impose charges for use of waterworks system services based on the provisions of the Municipal Revenue Code.
observations.reason: (21) The is still using the Municipal Revenue Code of 2005 as provided under Section 191 of RA 7160 to impose a more realistic rate that could give additional income and maximize revenue sources. (22) Still, no contract between the consumers of the waterworks system services and the Office of the Municipal Mayor was executed for availing the waterworks system services. (23) Rates stipulated in the Municipal Revenue Code for the use of waterworks system services were not strictly imposed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: (24) Sangguniang Bayan come up with clear policy guidelines on the hiring of personnel under Job Order Contracts and the LGU to strictly implement the guidelines.
observations.reason: No copy of the guidelines for the hiring of personnel under Job Order Contracts was submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: (25) Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking disbursement vouchers and prepare the subsidiary ledgers of the Due to BIR account. (26) Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with the respective deadlines for the remittance of withheld taxes set forth in BIR Revenue Regulations No. 6-2001 dated July 31, 2001 and BIR Memorandum Circular No. 23-2007 dated March 20, 2007.
observations.reason: (25) Lacking disbursement vouchers were not submitted and the MA failed to prepare subsidiary ledgers. (26) There were still delayed remittances noted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: (29) the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person and its Technical Working Group to speed up the preparation of monitoring tools such as the HGDG and PIMME to strengthen the determination, implementation and evaluation of gender related issues within the municipality as required under JMC No. 2013-01. (30) require the concerned department to enforce full implementation of the GAD related programs and likewise require the Municipal Accountant-Designate to submit the details of the disbursement pertaining to the actual cost incurred in the implementation of GAD related programs, projects and activities.
observations.reason: (29) The aforesaid monitoring tools were not prepared. (30) GAD PPAs were not fully implemented as of CY 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: (31) the Municipal Treasurer be required to maintain property cards, and the Municipal Accountant to maintain PPE ledger cards. (33) Both offices should reconcile their records regularly. (34) cause the conduct of complete physical inventory of all the properties and submit a report thereon.
observations.reason: (31) Property cards were still not maintained by the Municipal Treasurer. (32) Municipal Accountant still was not able to maintain PPELC. (33) Still no reconciliation was conducted. (34) No physical count was conducted; hence no report was submitted to the audit team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: (35) the Municipal Accountant-Designate consider the salvage value of the depreciable assets in computing for the depreciation and be mindful of the criteria set forth in the aforementioned guidelines as to when depreciation shall commence.
observations.reason: Lapsing schedule was not submitted for review and verification

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: (39) the Local School Board prepare an Annual School Board Budget that is properly planned and responsive to school needs, like the priority improvement of educational facilities and other relevant projects/programs/activities in accordance with Section 100 of Republic Act No. 7160 and DepED-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017.
observations.reason: Annual school board budget for CY 2019 was not submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: (46) the LGU observe strictly the prescribed employee’s minimum net take home pay under Section 47 of the General Provisions of the General Appropriations Act of 2017.
observations.reason: Still, there were employees with monthly take home pay below the minimum.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management submit the list and details of all on-going government projects/programs/activities and those that are to be implemented during the year details as required under COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.
observations.reason: The Municipal Engineer still do not comply with the quarterly submission of status of PPAs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management formulate an action plan to implement the audit recommendations, and submit immediately the duly accomplished AAPSI form to the audit team; copy furnished the DBM, the House Committee on Appropriations and the Senate Committee on Finance, as mandated by Section 96 of RA 10717.
observations.reason: Still no submission of the AAPSI within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: (58) the BAC Secretariat be required to submit to the audit team within the prescribed period copies of approved contracts/purchase orders within 5 days after their execution/issuance as provided for in COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
observations.reason: Copy of POs and contracts were still not submitted within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: (59) the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledgers to support the balances of the receivable accounts.
observations.reason: No subsidiaries maintained by the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: (64) the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant be directed to strictly observe the timely submission of the accounts.
observations.reason: Submission of accounts and reports were still delayed.

observations.recommendation:
1.5 We recommended and the management agreed that the LCE:
a. Create an inventory committee and direct them to conduct a physical
count of road network systems and prepare the Report on the Physical
Count of Local Road Network.
b. Require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a Report on Local Road
Networks every year-end.
c. Require he Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary records for
roads and road components for every road network and prepare the
lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation. Also, provide
the required disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements for road
networks.

2.7 We recommended and the management agreed to require the Municipal
Accountant to prepare Subsidiary Ledgers to support the balances of the Assets
totaling P5,021,415.21 and Liabilities totaling P46,333,326.55 for the ensuing
year, and submit the same to the Audit Team for review purposes.

3.10 We recommended and the management agreed to require the:
a. Municipal Assessor to prepare and submit the assessment roll to the
Municipal Treasurer, on or before the thirty-first (31st) day of December
each year.
b. Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit the certified list showing the
name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year, to the
Municipal Accountant, at the beginning of each year.
c. Municipal Accountant to set-up the Real Property Tax Receivable and
Special Education Tax Receivable at the beginning of each year, and
further to prepare journal entries for the unrecorded receivables for CY
2019.

4.6 We recommended and the management agreed to oblige:
1. The Municipal Budget Officer to strictly observe the proper charging of
expenditures to the expense item provided in the Annual Budget; and
2. The Municipal Accountant to record the wages of job order personnel in
the appropriate MOOE account titles in accordance with Annex B of COA
Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015.

5.10 We recommended that Management immediately submit the MOA or Trust
Agreement together with Local Sanggunian Resolution authorizing the LCE to
enter into a contract for the fund transfer from DILG amounting to
	3,000,000.00. Henceforth, ensure that the COA Audit Team is always
furnished a copy of the mentioned documents necessary in the audit of the
receipt and utilization of the Trust Fund, as well as in the evaluation of project
implementation.

6.7 We recommended that Management fill in the Plantilla Positions or designate a
permanent staff in concurrent capacity as required by the existing regulations
who will perform the functions of administration and training, research and
planning and operations and warning.

7.7 We recommended that the LCE require the members of the GAD Focal Point
System to:
e. Conduct gender analysis and administer the use of HGDG tool to
determine the cost to be attributed to GAD in the implementation
of LGUs major PPAs during GAD PB preparation.
f. Employ the use of HGDG test using PIMME checklist or FIMME
checklist to determine actual expenditure that can be attributed to
the GAD in the GAD Accomplishment Report.
g. Submit documentation of projects with no cost incurred as proof
that they were actually implemented.
h. Strengthen the GAD Focal Point System and immediately establish
the GAD database of the LGU.

7.8 We also recommended that the Accountant be required to submit the details of
the disbursements related to implementation of GAD PPAs as reported in the
GAR.

8.6 We recommended and the management agreed that the Municipal Budget
Officer be obliged to maintain the required registries of appropriations,
allotments and obligations, for fiscal and internal control purposes.

8.7 We further recommended and the management agreed that the Budget Officer
be required to prepare and submit the Statement of Appropriation, Allotment
and Obligation to the Accountant, and the latter to prepare and submit the
Statement of Comparison Budget and Actual Amounts.

9.4 We recommended and the management agreed that the Municipal Accountant
and Municipal Treasurer be required to ensure that all payment for
infrastructure projects are subjected to correct computation of the mandatory
5% VAT and 2% Income Tax and remit the same to the BIR within the
prescribed period.
We recommended that the: (7) Municipal Accountant be required to provide an allowance for depreciation on the LGU’s depreciable assets for fair presentation of the financial statements.
observations.reason: Lapsing Schedule was not submitted for verification

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor facilitate the opening of Special Trust Account and require: (9) immediately transfer the unexpended LDRRMF for CY 2018 amounting to ₱3,167,662.07 and those from prior years; (10) provide disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements the appropriations, disbursement and unexpended balance of LDRRMF for the current year and those under the special trust fund which represent the unexpended balances from previous years; (11) immediately submit the lacking documents pertaining to the above-mentioned disbursements and henceforth, ensure that transactions are supported with complete documentary requirements before payments be made; (12) make an adjusting entry and corresponding explanation to correct the previous entry made as follows: Debit: Medical, Dental and Laboratory Supplies for Distribution ₱186,048.93 Credit: Government Equity ₱186,048.93 Debit: Food Supplies Inventory ₱283,709.00 Credit: Government Equity ₱283,709.00 To record the correcting entry to recognize the Inventories Account that were previously recognized as expense. Debit: Disaster Response and Equipment ₱176,750.00 Credit: Motor Vehicle ₱176,750.00 To record reclassification of entry made on the purchase of Motor Vehicle to Disaster Response and Equipment Account. (13) maintain the required registries and controls pertaining to the LDRRMF for the current and previous years; (14) Enforce the LDRRMC to annually prepare and update the LDRRMFIP detailing the projects to be funded by the 70% mitigation funds, 30% QRF in lump-sum amount and the specific projects to be funded by the unexpended funds from prior years in a separate caption; (15) Moreover, ensure full implementation of the projects included in the LDRRMFIP to lessen adverse impact of hazards and full benefits will be obtained by the constituents of the municipality.
observations.reason: (9) Still, the unexpended LDRRMF balances were not transferred to a Special Trust Fund Account. (10) No disclosure was made in the Notes to Financial Statements. (11) Still not submitted as of the report date. (12) No adjusting entry was reflected in the concerned general ledger account titles. (13) The required registries and controls were not maintained. (14) Prior years’ unexpended balances were not included in the preparation of the LDRRMFIP. (15) Still, PPAs under the LDRRMFIP were not fully realized as of CY 2019 year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: (20) oblige the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to follow-up the approval status of its Ten-Year Solid Waste Management Plan and thereafter, comply with the recommendations made by National Solid Waste Management Commission in the implementation of such plan.
observations.reason: The MSWMB followed-up the approval of the Ten-Year Solid Waste Management Plan. However, thereafter, comply with the recommendations as of the report date.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive oblige: (27) The Municipal Accountant to remit the withheld funds to the concerned government agencies on time and intact. (28) Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking vouchers and make the necessary adjusting entries to correct the balances of the Due to GSIS, Pag-Ibig and PhilHealth accounts.
observations.reason: (27) There were still delayed remittances to concerned agencies noted. (28) Lacking disbursement vouchers were not submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: (36) strictly enforce the preparation of all documents to support the DV. No trip tickets for trips within the municipality.
observations.reason: Only the Monthly Consumption Report was attached to the DV. No trip tickets for trips within the municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: (37) Henceforth, strictly observe the timely submission of the accounts and financial reports in accordance with laws, rules and regulations. (38) Henceforth, strictly observe the timely submission of the accounts and required reports in accordance with laws, rules and regulations.
observations.reason: (37) The 2017 and 2018 unsubmitted accounts were partially complied and Notice of Suspension was issued for the unsubmitted accounts for CY 2019. (38) Submission of accounts and reports were still delayed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: (40) Require all officials and employees to liquidate all outstanding cash advances immediately. (41) Require the Municipal Accountant-Designate to strictly implement the guidelines set forth under COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 89 of P.D. 1445, monitor and demand settlement of overdue cash advances before additional cash advance will be granted. (42) Require the Municipal Treasurer to attach a summary of payrolls for every cash advance and to indicate in the cash disbursement report the reference or details to which the liquidation of cash advance specifically applies.
observations.reason: (40) There were still long outstanding cash advances. (41) Lacking proof of compliance.. (42) There were still cash advances not supported with payrolls and liquidations do not have check references.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: (43) the Municipal Planning and Development Council (MPDC), through the Municipal Mayor as Chairman to include in the Annual Investment Plan the projects which can readily be implemented by the LGU during the year and the Municipal Engineer to fully implement the projects contained in the AIP, through proper scheduling and prioritization of the projects to ensure optimal utilization of the 20% Development Fund. (44) Also, guidelines on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund as provided for in DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 must be strictly observed. (45) Moreover, we recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Accountant-Designate to submit immediately lacking supporting and unsubmitted disbursement vouchers pertaining to the 20% Development Fund.
observations.reason: (43) 20% DF for CY 2019 were still underutilized. (44) Guidelines on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund were implemented. (45) Proof of submission was not submitted to the current Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: (47) the designated Municipal Accountant be required to prepare and submit the Statement of Financial Performance for the year ended December 31, 2016 under Trust Fund, Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts under the General Fund, and (48) to disclose the details of some line items in the Statement of Cash Flows and (49) prepare the Reconciliation of Net Cash Flows from Operating Activities to Surplus/Deficit as required under PPSAS No. 1.
observations.reason: (47) No SCBAA for CY 2019. (48) No reconciliation required for CY 2019. (49) No reconciliation was prepared.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management submit all lacking documents as indicated above, and to strictly comply with the provisions set forth under COA Circular 2012-01 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended: (51) the submission of the documents called for in COA, DBM, DILG, GCC, and DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015 to support the cash advances for the confidential fund. (52) Henceforth, strictly comply with the timeframe for the submission of DV with complete supporting documents as provided for in the Joint Circular.
observations.reason: (51) There were still unliquidated funds. (52) Still delay in submission.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: (55) the concerned officers and employees be required to liquidate immediately their outstanding cash advances in accordance with Section 14 of EO 298 dated March 23, 2004 and COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997. (56) that no travel advances shall be granted unless previous ones have been liquidated pursuant to Section 89 of PD 1445 and Section 4.1.2 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997. (57) Likewise, require the Municipal Accountant to provide schedules or details to support the unliquidated balance and make the necessary disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.reason: (55) Several employees are still with unliquidated cash advance as of the report date. (56) Several employees are still granted with additional cash advance. (57) No disclosures were made for CY 2019 Notes to FS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: (60) management strictly adhere to the processes outlined in the IRR of RA 9184 in the procurement of government projects. (61) Likewise, see to it that Pakyaw Contract Agreement shall include only the labor component of the projects undertaken by administration.
observations.reason: (60) Management partially complied with the procurement process under RA NO. 9184. (61) Implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: (62) the Municipal Accountant record the goods purchased as Inventory following the perpetual inventory method. (63) Likewise, Issuances thereof shall be recorded as expense as they take place and a Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) shall be prepared and submitted to the Municipal Accountant for recording in the books.
observations.reason: (62) Some of the procured inventories were recognized as inventory. (63) No SSMI were being maintained by the Municipal Accountant-Designate.

observations.recommendation:
1.8 We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to:
1. Prepare the subsidiary ledgers of the Cash in Bank – Local Currency,
Current Account;
2. Prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statements on all the LGU bank accounts
and submit the bank statements of the four bank accounts; and
3. Henceforth, prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statements for all bank
accounts and submit the same together with the bank statements, paid
checks, debit/credit memos and other related documents.

2.15 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require:
1. All Revenue Collection Clerks to remit to the Municipal Treasurer the full
amount of their collections daily and intact, regardless of the nature of
collections made;
2. The Municipal Treasurer to deposit regularly and intact all his collections
and those remitted by his collectors within the reglementary period,
particularly when the collections reach P10,000.00 pursuant to Section
69(1) of PD 1445 and Section 28 of COA Circular No. 92-382.
3. The Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile their
records regularly to produce reliable reports.

3.7 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be required to provide an
allowance for depreciation on the LGU’s depreciable assets for fair
presentation of accounts in the financial statements.
3.8 The Municipal Accountant-Designate could not provide that allowance for
depreciation due to absence of reports and registries pertaining to the assets owned
by the municipality.

4.4 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review thoroughly the
entries made to its Loans Payable – Domestic account and prepare the
necessary correcting entries thereafter.

5.14 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor facilitate the opening of Special
Trust Account and require:
1. The Municipal Accountant-Designate to:
a) immediately transfer the unexpended LDRRMF for CY 2018
amounting to ₱3,167,662.07 and those from prior years;
b) provide disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statement the
appropriations, disbursement and unexpended balance of LDRRMF
for the current year and those under the special trust fund which
represent the unexpended balances from previous years;
c) immediately submit the lacking documents pertaining to the above
mentioned disbursements and henceforth, ensure that transactions
are supported with complete documentary requirements before
payments be made;
d) make an adjusting entry and corresponding explanation to correct the
previous entry made as follows:
Debit: Medical, Dental and Laboratory
Supplies for Distribution ₱186,048.93
Credit: Government Equity ₱186,048.93
Debit: Food Supplies Inventory ₱283,709.00
Credit: Government Equity ₱283,709.00
To record the correcting entry to recognize the Inventories Account
that were previously recognized as expense.
Debit: Disaster Response and Equipment ₱176,750.00
Credit: Motor Vehicle ₱176,750.00
To record reclassification of entry made on the purchase of Motor Vehicle
to Disaster Response and Equipment Account.
e) maintain the required registries and controls pertaining to the
LDRRMF for the current and previous years
2. Enforce the LDRRMC to annually prepare and update the LDRRMFIP
detailing the projects to be funded by the 70% mitigation funds, 30% QRF
in lump-sum amount and the specific projects to be funded by the
unexpended funds from prior years in a separate caption;
3. Moreover, ensure full implementation of the projects included in the
LDRRMFIP to lessen adverse impact of hazards and full benefits will be
obtained by the constituents of the municipality.

6.11 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to withhold retention money from progress payments and ensure
that only claims with complete supporting documents are processed and paid.
Likewise, require the BAC and Municipal Accountant to oblige the concerned
contractor to post warranty security for the procurement of infrastructure
project that had already been fully paid. Henceforth, strictly observe the
mandatory posting of warranty security to guarantee that the contractor shall
perform his responsibilities pursuant to Section 62 of the IRR of RA 9184 and
Section 9.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

7.6 We recommended for the strict observance of the guidelines on the
appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund as provided for
in DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22,
2017.
7.7 The management agreed to adhere to the recommendation of the Audit Team for
the proper utilization of the 20% Development Fund.

8.7 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive oblige the Municipal Solid
Waste Management Board to follow-up the status of its Ten-Year Solid Waste
Management Plan and thereafter, comply with the recommendations made by
National Solid Waste Management Commission in the implementation of such
plan.

9.7 We recommended that:
1. The Sangguniang Bayan immediately revise the Municipal Revenue Code
of 2005 as provided under Section 191 of RA 7160 to impose a more
realistic rate that could give additional income and maximize revenue
sources.
2. The LGU make use of the water meters bought in CY 2016 and require
consumers to execute a written application/contract to be filed with the
Office of the Municipal Mayor for availing the waterworks system
services.
3. The Municipal Treasurer to impose charges for use of waterworks system
based on the provisions of the Municipal Revenue Code.

10.7 We recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan come up with a clear policy
guidelines on the hiring of personnel under Job Order Contracts and the LGU
to strictly implement the guidelines.

11.7 We recommended that the LCE oblige:
1. The Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking disbursement voucher
and prepare the subsidiary ledgers of the Due to BIR account.
2. The Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with the respective deadlines for
the remittance of withheld taxes set forth in BIR Revenue Regulations No. 6-
2001 dated July 31, 2001 and BIR Revenue Memorandum Circular 23-2007
dated March 20, 2007.

12.12 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive oblige:
1. The Municipal Treasurer to remit the withheld funds to the concerned
government agencies on time and intact.
2. The Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking disbursement voucher
and make the necessary adjusting entries to correct the balances of the
Due to GSIS, Pag-Ibig and PhilHealth accounts.

13.8 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person and
its Technical Working Group to speed up the preparation of monitoring tools
such as the HGDG and PIMME to strengthen the determination,
implementation and evaluation of gender related issues within the
municipality as required under JMC No. 2013-01. Moreover, require the
concerned department to enforce full implementation of the GAD related
programs and likewise require the Municipal Accountant-Designate to
submit the details of disbursement pertaining to the actual cost incurred in the
implementation of GAD related programs, projects and activities.
13.9 The GAD Focal Person commented that she was not yet oriented on the use of these
monitoring tools for the determination and implementation of GAD PPAs within
the municipality. She also said that there were GAD related seminars conducted by
68
various agencies which they were not informed of and thus hindered them to attend
the seminar. They ensure that they will put on some efforts to participate to GAD
related seminars to employ these tools in the municipality.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant be required to regularly
prepare the Bank reconciliation Statements and submit the same to the audit
team for verification pursuant to the provisions of Section 74 of P.D. No. 1445
and Sections 3.2, 3.3 and 3.4 of COA Circular No. 96-001 dated October 2, 1996.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer be required to maintain
property cards and the Municipal Accountant to maintain PPE ledger cards.
Both offices should reconcile their records regularly. Likewise, cause the
conduct of complete physical inventory of all the Municipality’s properties and
submit a report thereon.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant - Designate consider
the salvage value of the depreciable assets in computing for the depreciation
expense and be mindful of the criteria set forth in the aforementioned guidelines
as to when depreciation shall commence.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be required to transfer
the unexpended LDRRMF totaling ₱3,887,495.35 to the Special Trust Fund.
Moreover, require the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer
to prepare and submit LDRRMFIP as required under Section 5.1.2 of COA
Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
1. Required all officials and employees to liquidate all outstanding cash
advances immediately.
2. Required the Municipal Accountant-Designate to
(a) strictly implement the guidelines set forth under COA Circular
97-002 and Section 89 of P.D. 1445, monitor and demand
settlement of overdue cash advances before additional cash
advance will be granted
(b) submit all the lacking documents such as DTRs, Accomplishment
Reports and Job Order Contracts
3. Required the Municipal Treasurer to attach a summary of payrolls
for every cash advance and to indicate in the cash disbursement
report the reference or details to which the liquidation of cash
advance specifically applies.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Treasurer-designate and Municipal Accountant-designate to submit the
disbursement vouchers to support the recorded check disbursements for
January to December totaling ₱9,083,271.59 and the 238 reported cancelled
checks. Henceforth, strictly observe the timely submission of the accounts and
financial reports in accordance with laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development
Council (MPDC), through the Municipal Mayor as Chairman to include in the
Annual Investment Plan the projects which can readily be implemented by the
LGU during the year and the Municipal Engineer to fully implement the
projects contained in the AIP, through proper scheduling and prioritization of
the projects to ensure optimal utilization of the 20% Development Fund. Also,
guidelines on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund
as provided for in DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated
February 22, 2017 must be strictly observed.

Moreover, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the
Accountant-Designate to submit immediately lacking supporting and
unsubmitted disbursement vouchers pertaining to the 20% Development Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant – Designate and Treasurer to observe the respective deadlines set
forth in Section 14 of the IRR of RA No. 8291, PhilHealth Circular No. 0001 s.
2014, and Section E item 4 of HDMF Circular No. 275 on its GSIS, PhilHealth
and Pag-IBIG remittances, to avoid interests and penalties.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor strictly enforce the
preparation of all the necessary documents to support the disbursements for
fuel and lubricants in order to properly control and regulate its use strictly for
vehicles owned by the LGU.

We recommended that the Local School Board prepare an Annual
School Board Budget that is properly planned and responsive to the school
needs, like the priority improvement of educational facilities and other relevant
projects/programs/activities in accordance with Section 100 of Republic Act No.
7160 and DepED-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Local
Finance Committee to include in the Annual Budget an appropriation equivalent
to 1% of IRA to finance Local Council for the Protection of Children activities,
cause the creation of LCPC and adopt a comprehensive plan and program for
the welfare of the children.

We recommended for the creation of the Municipal Solid Waste
Management Board; the preparation of a 10-year Municipal Solid Waste
Management Plan; and the implementation of the projects programmed during
the current year.

We recommended that the LGU observe strictly the prescribed
minimum employee’s monthly net take home pay under Section 47 of the General
Provisions of the General Appropriations Act of 2017.

We recommended that the designated Municipal Accountant be required to prepare and submit the Statement of Financial Performance for the year ended December 31, 2016 under the Trust Fund, Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts under the General Fund, and to disclose the details of some line items in the Statement of Cash Flows and prepare the required Reconciliation of Net Cash Flows from Operating Activities to Surplus/Deficit and Reconciliation of Budget and Actual Amounts.
observations.reason: acquainting the PPSAS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be required to maintain a separate registries and subsidiary records for LDRRMF. Likewise, require the LDRRMO to submit an LDRRMF Plan.
observations.reason: Only utilization of LDRRMF for CY 2017 was submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management submit all lacking documents as indicated above, and to strictly comply with the provisions set forth under COA Circular 2012-01 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.reason: Not implemented. The required documents were not yet submitted for audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant be required to submit the accounts and financial reports and to strictly observe the timely submission of the same in accordance with Section 347 of republic Act 7160, Section7.2.1 (a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009, and Section 39 of PD1445.

We recommended for the submission of the documents called for in COA, DBM, DILG, GCC, and DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015 to support the cash advances for the confidential fund. Henceforth, strictly comply with the submission of DV with the complete supporting documents as provided for in the Joint Circular.
observations.reason: Not implemented. The required documents were not yet submitted for audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management submit the list and details of all on-going government projects/programs/activities and those that are to be implemented during the year pursuant to COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.
observations.reason: However, compliance on the postings of signboards could not be confirmed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management formulate an action plan to implement the recommendations, and submit AAPSI.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be required to present properly the items on the face of the financial statements and to disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the required Reconciliation of Net Cash Flows from Operating Activities to Surplus/Deficit, Reconciliation of Budget And Actual Amounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statements and submit the same to the audit team for verification pursuant to the provisions of Section 74 of P.D. 1445 and Section 3.2 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.
observations.reason: They will start the bank reconciliation for this current year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer be required to maintain property cards and the Municipal Accountant to maintain PPE ledger cards. Both offices should reconcile their records regularly. Likewise, cause the conduct of complete physical inventory of all the Municipality’s properties and submit a report thereon.
observations.reason: They will request previous municipal treasurer for records of it.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be required to provide an allowance for depreciation on all of the municipality’s depreciable assets in accordance with PPSAS 17.
observations.reason: Computation of depreciation was not based on one month after acquisition date. Or on that month, if bought earlier during the month.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that all collection clerks be required to turnover their collections daily to the Municipal Treasurer, and for the latter to remit his collections daily and intact to the depository bank in accordance with Section 307 of RA 7160.
observations.reason: Partially implemented. For CY 2017 observed remittances was made. However, collection clerks still observed delayed deposits.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that all concerned officers and employees be required to liquidate immediately their outstanding cash travel advances in accordance with Section 14 of EO 298 and Section 4.1.2 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997. Likewise, require the Municipal Accountant to provide schedules or details to support the unliquidated balance and make the necessary disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.reason: Not yet liquidated. See CY 2017 Finding No. 9.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management provide a budget for GAD equivalent to at least 5% of the Municipality’s total appropriation and formulate GAD Plan for CY 2015 as required in Section34 of the General Provisions of the General Appropriations Act of 2015 and Section4.4 of DBM, NEDA and NCRFW Joint Circular No. 2004-1 dated April 5, 2004.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be required to present properly line items on the face of the financial statements and to disclose in the Notes to the Financial Statements the required Reconciliation of Net Cash Flows from Operating Activities to Surplus/Deficit and Reconciliation of Budget and Actual Amounts.
observations.reason: Partially implemented. Municipal registries of LDRRMF yet.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC Secretariat be required to submit to the audit team copies of approved contracts/purchase orders within five days after their execution/issuance as provided for in COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
observations.reason: Submission of copies of contracts/POs to audit team was not made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledgers to support the general ledger balances of the receivable accounts.
observations.reason: No SL maintained for all receivable accounts. They will try to maintain for current year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management strictly observe the guidelines on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% appropriation of the annual internal revenue allotment for development projects.
observations.reason: For CY 2017, ₱1,080,00.00 worth of ineligible projects/program was incurred by the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management strictly adhere to the processes outlined in the IRR of RA 9184 in the procurement of government projects. Likewise, see to it that the Pakyaw Contract Agreement shall include only the labor component of the projects undertaken by administration.
observations.reason: To fully implement in present year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the goods purchased as Inventory following the perpetual inventory method. Likewise, issuances thereof shall be recorded as expense as they take place and a Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) shall be prepared and submitted to the Municipal Accountant for recording in the books.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Revenue Collection Clerks to turnover their collections daily to the Municipal Treasurer, and for the latter to deposit all his collections as well as collections turned over to him by the Revenue Collection Clerks with authorized depository bank daily or not later than the next banking day.
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant be directed to strictly observe the timely submission of their reports and accounts.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should strictly comply with the provisions of Section 74 of P.D. 1445 and Section 3.2 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

We recommended the Mayor to direct all concerned officers and employees to immediately liquidate the outstanding cash advances, and henceforth, to strictly implement the provisions of Sec. 89 of P.D. 1445, COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997. Also recommend the Municipal Mayor to direct the designated Municipal Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher to reclassify the unliquidated cash advances to the proper account.
observations.reason: Liquidations were submitted. See CY 2017 Finding No. 9.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should direct all concerned to strictly observe the provisions of COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with the pertinent provisions of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for Local Government Units, Volumes I and II.

We recommend that the AAR, Municipal Accountant be required to present properly line items on the face of the financial statements and to disclose in the Notes to the Consolidated Notes to the Financial Statements the Reconciliation of Net Cash Flows from Operating Activities to Surplus/Deficit, and Reconciliation Between the Actual Budget and Actual Amounts.
observations.managementAction: The municipal accountant is acquainting herself with the PPSAS

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant be required to regularly prepare the bank Reconciliation Statements and submit the same to the audit team for verification pursuant to the provisions of Section 74 of P.D. 1445 and Sections 3.2, 3.3 and 3.4 of COA Circular No. 96-001 dated October 2, 1996.
observations.managementAction: The municipal accountant will start the preparation of bank reconciliation statements for year 2016, then work back for prior years.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer be required to maintain property records and the Municipal Accountant to maintain PPE ledger cards. Both offices should reconcile their records regularly. Likewise, cause of conduct of complete physical inventory of all the Municipality’s properties and submit a report thereon.
observations.managementAction: LGU to request the previous municipal treasurer for PPE cards and they will conduct physical count of assets then maintain records of it.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant be required to provide an allowance for depreciation for all of the Municipality’s depreciable assets in accordance with PPSAS 17.
observations.managementAction: The Accountant will try to start to maintain the allowance for depreciation for all depreciable assets.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the concerned officers and employees be required to liquidate immediately their unliquidated travel advances in accordance with Section 14 of Executive Order No. 298 dated March 23, 2004 and that no travel advances shall be granted unless previous ones have been liquidated pursuant to Section 89 of P.D. 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997. Likewise, require the Municipal Accountant to provide schedules or details to support the unliquidated balance and make the necessary disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: The municipal accountant shall secure the list of all the names of officials and employees with all the names of officials and employees with travel advances.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management provide a budget for GAD equivalent to at least 5% of the Municipality’s total appropriation and formulate GAD Plan as required in Section 34 of the General Provisions and DBM, NEDA and NCRFW Joint Circular No. 2004-11 dated April 5, 2004.

We recommend that the BAC Secretariat be required to submit copies of approved contracts/purchase orders within 5 days after their execution/issuance as provided for in COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
observations.managementAction: Agency promised to submit for current year.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management formulate an action plan to implement the recommendations, and submit the duly accomplished AAPSI form to the Auditor, copy furnished the DBM, the House Committee on Appropriations and Senate Committee on Finance pursuant to Section 93 of R.A. 10651.
observations.managementAction: Agency promised to submit for current year. See related audit discussion in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledgers to support the general ledger balances of the receivable accounts.
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant should strictly comply with the provisions of Section 74 of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and Section 3.2 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.
observations.managementAction: They will start to have a bank reconciliation this current year.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the municipal treasurer’s offices properly maintain property records and to conduct regular reconciliation of both records so that errors could be detected and necessary adjustments be made to come up with correct balances; create a committee to conduct an inventory of all properties of the government; and send concerned officials and employees to attend trainings on Property Management.
observations.managementAction: They will start to prepare property records after actual inventory is made.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant should provide depreciation for all Property, Plant and Equipment items.
observations.managementAction: They will provide depreciation for all depreciable assets.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor should direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with the pertinent provisions of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for Local Government Units, Volumes I and II.
observations.managementAction: Registries are in line with PPSAS they will try to accomplish the same.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the designated Municipal Accountant be required to
prepare and submit the Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual
Amounts, and disclose in the Notes to Combined Financial Statements, the
details of some line items in the Combined Statement of Cash Flows and the
Reconciliation of Net Cash Flows from Operating Activities to Surplus/Deficit
as required under Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) No.
1.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant be required to maintain a
separate registries and subsidiary records for the LDRRMF and submit to COA
a Report on the Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF. Likewise, require the
LDRRMO to submit a LDRRM Plan.

We recommend that management submit all lacking documents and to
strictly comply with the provisions set forth under COA Circular 2012-01 dated
June 14, 2012 and RA 9184 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant
be required to submit the accounts and financial reports and to strictly
observe the timely submission of the same in accordance with Section 347 of
republic Act 7160, Section7.2.1 (a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated
September 15, 2009, and Section 39 of PD1445. This recommendation is
without prejudice to the result of post audit of the accounts and financial
reports.

We recommend for the submission of the documents called for in COA,
DBM, DILG, GCC, and DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8,
2015 to support the cash advances for the confidential fund. Henceforth,
strictly comply with the timeframe for the submission of DV with the
complete supporting documents as provided for in the Joint Circular.

We recommend that management submit the list and details of all on-going
government projects/programs/activities and those that are to be
implemented during the year pursuant to COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated
January 30, 2013.
