We recommend that management formulate an action plan to implement
the audit recommendations, and submit immediately the duly accomplished
AAPSI form to the audit team; copy furnished the DBM, the House
Committee on Appropriations and the Senate Committee on Finance, as
mandated by Section 96 of RA 10717.
We recommend that all collection clerks be required to turnover their collections daily to the Municipal Treasurer and also for the latter to remit his collections daily to the municipal treasurer.
observations.managementAction: Collectors are now remitting their collections weekly to the municipal treasurer.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive confers with the management of LBP- Catbalogan Branch for the return of excess remittance of loan amortization of the employee borrower. Henceforth, stop the practice of remitting employees loan amortization based on bank’s billing statement but rather it should be based on the actual amount deducted from employees’ payroll. Likewise, require the Municipal Accountant to analyze the Due to Other GOCCs account, determine the other causes of negative balance, and adjust the books accordingly.
observations.managementAction: LGU reported to the AAR that they reconciled the difference. Henceforth, stop the practice of remitting employees loan amortization based on bank's billing statement but rather it should be based on the actual amount deducted from employees’ payroll.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Mayor confers with the management of HDMF to request for the removal of the excess remittance of contributions from the employee’s records and to consider the over-remittance as advance payment of government share premium contributions of the Municipality of San Jose de Buan.
observations.managementAction: The LGU has already reconciled the difference. Henceforth, stop the practice.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant be required to transfer the unexpended/unobligated balance of the DRRM to the Special Trust Fund, and henceforth, render a proper accounting and reporting of the DRRM Fund.
observations.managementAction: Will open Trust Fund in July 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly observe the guidelines on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% annual internal revenue allotment for development projects.
observations.reason: The agency shall identify what particular items or projects were not qualified for the 20% DF utilization.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management limit the appropriations and expenditures for personal services to 55% of the total annual income from regular sources of the next preceding fiscal year so as not to prejudice the priority needs of the municipality and the community as a whole.
observations.managementAction: LGU reported to AAR that they have complied all mandatory position funded which is exempted from 55% PS.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly adhere to the processes outlined in the IRR of RA 9184 in the procurement of government projects. Likewise, see to it that Pakyaw Contract Agreement shall include only the administration labor component of the projects.
observations.managementAction: The LGU has already implemented the IRR of RA 9184. To fully implement in present year.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant record the goods purchased as Inventory following the perpetual inventory method. Likewise, issuances thereof shall be recorded as expense as they take place and a Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) shall be prepared and submitted to the Municipal Accountant for recording in the books.
observations.managementAction: They will use the perpetual inventory method. Likewise, issuances thereof shall be recorded as expense as they take place.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Revenue Collection Clerks to turnover their collections daily to the Municipal Treasurer, and for the latter to deposit all his collections by the authorized depository bank daily or not later than the next banking day.
observations.managementAction: See related discussion in Finding No. 5, Revenue Collection Finding No. 8 this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant be directed to strictly observe the timely submission of the municipal accounts.
observations.reason: The lack of signatures from officials causes the delay of the submission of the accounts. See related discussion in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Mayor should direct all concerned officers and employees to immediately liquidate the outstanding cash advances, and henceforth, to strictly implement the provisions of Sec. 89 of P.D. 1445, COA Circ. No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997. We also recommend that the Municipal Mayor should direct the designated Municipal Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher to reclassify the unliquidated cash advances to the proper account in order to come up with correct account balances.
observations.reason: Still with outstanding unliquidated cash advances.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Mayor direct all concerned to strictly observe the provisions of COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977.
observations.reason: They will look on the trip tickets to account for the monthly fuel consumption.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Engineer, Municipal Budget Officer and all concerned should strictly implement the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.
observations.reason: The municipality will do its best to provide the auditor with this report as promised during the exit conference.
